speaker: ANTONY
text: Yet they are not joined. Where yond pine does stand,

speaker: ANTONY
text: I shall discover all. I’ll bring thee word

speaker: ANTONY
text: Straight how ’tis like to go.

speaker: SCARUS
text: Swallows have built

speaker: SCARUS
text: In Cleopatra’s sails their nests. The augurs

speaker: SCARUS
text: Say they know not, they cannot tell, look grimly

speaker: SCARUS
text: And dare not speak their knowledge. Antony

speaker: SCARUS
text: Is valiant and dejected, and by starts

speaker: SCARUS
text: His fretted fortunes give him hope and fear

speaker: SCARUS
text: Of what he has and has not.

speaker: ANTONY
text: All is lost!

speaker: ANTONY
text: This foul Egyptian hath betrayèd me.

speaker: ANTONY
text: My fleet hath yielded to the foe, and yonder

speaker: ANTONY
text: They cast their caps up and carouse together

speaker: ANTONY
text: Like friends long lost. Triple-turned whore! ’Tis thou

speaker: ANTONY
text: Hast sold me to this novice, and my heart

speaker: ANTONY
text: Makes only wars on thee. Bid them all fly—

speaker: ANTONY
text: For when I am revenged upon my charm,

speaker: ANTONY
text: I have done all. Bid them all fly. Begone!

speaker: ANTONY
text: O sun, thy uprise shall I see no more.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Fortune and Antony part here; even here

speaker: ANTONY
text: Do we shake hands. All come to this? The hearts

speaker: ANTONY
text: That spanieled me at heels, to whom I gave

speaker: ANTONY
text: Their wishes, do discandy, melt their sweets

speaker: ANTONY
text: On blossoming Caesar, and this pine is barked

speaker: ANTONY
text: That overtopped them all. Betrayed I am.

speaker: ANTONY
text: O, this false soul of Egypt! This grave charm,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Whose eye becked forth my wars and called them home,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Whose bosom was my crownet, my chief end,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Like a right gypsy hath at fast and loose

speaker: ANTONY
text: Beguiled me to the very heart of loss.—

speaker: ANTONY
text: What Eros, Eros!

speaker: ANTONY
text: Ah, thou spell! Avaunt!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Why is my lord enraged against his love?

speaker: ANTONY
text: Vanish, or I shall give thee thy deserving

speaker: ANTONY
text: And blemish Caesar’s triumph. Let him take thee

speaker: ANTONY
text: And hoist thee up to the shouting plebeians!

speaker: ANTONY
text: Follow his chariot, like the greatest spot

speaker: ANTONY
text: Of all thy sex; most monster-like be shown

speaker: ANTONY
text: For poor’st diminutives, for dolts, and let

speaker: ANTONY
text: Patient Octavia plow thy visage up

speaker: ANTONY
text: With her preparèd nails.

speaker: ANTONY
text: ’Tis well th’ art gone,

speaker: ANTONY
text: If it be well to live. But better ’twere

speaker: ANTONY
text: Thou fell’st into my fury, for one death

speaker: ANTONY
text: Might have prevented many.—Eros, ho!—

speaker: ANTONY
text: The shirt of Nessus is upon me. Teach me,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Alcides, thou mine ancestor, thy rage.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Let me lodge Lichas on the horns o’ th’ moon,

speaker: ANTONY
text: And with those hands that grasped the heaviest club

speaker: ANTONY
text: Subdue my worthiest self. The witch shall die.

speaker: ANTONY
text: To the young Roman boy she hath sold me, and I fall

speaker: ANTONY
text: Under this plot. She dies for ’t.—Eros, ho!
